creator: Andrew Widdowson

evidence.text: Postmortems (see [Postmortem Culture: Learning from Failure](https://sre.google/sre-book/postmortem-culture/)) are an important part of continuous improvement. They are a blame-free way of getting at the many root causes of a significant or visible outage. When writing a postmortem, keep in mind that its most appreciative audience might be an engineer who hasn’t yet been hired. Without radical editing, subtle changes can be made to our best postmortems to make them "teachable" postmortems.

provenance: Public first-party Google SRE book chapter; bounded source evidence retained.
relevance_evidence: The source documents structured team learning, postmortem study, and shared operational knowledge.
rights_note: Only a bounded publisher-authored excerpt is retained.
title: Accelerating SREs to On-Call and Beyond
